"まま" means "as is" or "unchanged".
"〜うちに" means "while" or "before" something changes.
"〜ように" can express purpose, like "in order to" or "so that".
"〜やすい" attaches to verb stems to mean "easy to do".
"〜にくい" attaches to verb stems to mean "hard to do".
「にする」 means "decide on" a choice or selection.
"「わけではない" means "not necessarily" or "it's not that".
"〜おかげで" expresses gratitude for a cause, "thanks to".
"〜ようにする" means "make an effort to...".
"〜にしては" means "considering...".
"〜ようにして" means "in order to" or "so that" (practice variation 34).
"～に向けて" means "toward / in preparation for".

"〜以来(いらい)" means "since (doing)".
"〜ようです" means "it seems like" and is based on observation. (practice variation 10)
"〜かもしれない" means "might be" or "maybe". (practice variation 39)
〜そうです attaches to verbs/adjectives to report hearsay (I heard that...). (practice variation 15)
"〜にする" means "decide on" a choice or selection. (practice variation 46)
"〜ば" forms a conditional meaning "if" in a general sense. (practice variation 21)
"〜まま" means "as is" or "unchanged". (practice variation 26)
"〜ても" means "even if" or "even though". (practice variation 22)
"〜てくれる" indicates someone does a favor for the speaker. (practice variation 15)
"〜にする" means "decide on" a choice or selection. (practice variation 46)
"〜ても" means "even if" or "even though".
"〜ように" can express purpose, like "in order to" or "so that". (practice variation 34)
"〜ことにする" expresses a decision, like "decide to".
"〜にすぎない" means "no more than..."
〜ばかりか" means "not only... but also".
"〜てしまう" can show completion or regret about an action. (practice variation 8)
"〜にする" means "decide on" a choice or selection.
"〜ことになる" indicates something was decided (by others or circumstances). (practice variation 37)
"〜ことができる" expresses ability, like "can do". (practice variation 41)
"〜なくてもいい" means "don't have to" and lifts an obligation. (practice variation 14)
"〜ば〜ほど" means "the more... the more...". (practice variation 55)
"〜によると" means "according to...".
"〜ことがある" means "sometimes" when talking about habitual actions.
"〜ておく" means "do in advance" or "leave as is".
"〜らしい" can mean "seems" or describe a typical characteristic.
"〜に応じて" means "depending on..."
〜ばかり means "just" (in terms of time) or "only" depending on context. (practice variation 27)
"〜てあげる" shows the speaker does a favor for someone else. (practice variation 17)
"〜ながらも" means "even while" or "though" (contrast).
"〜に加えて" means "in addition to...".
